message:
feat(harness): migrate golden checksums from SipHash to BLAKE3 with full visual state hashing

Replace the DefaultHasher (SipHash-1-3) checksum implementation with
BLAKE3 for all golden file verification in ftui-harness. This fixes
two fundamental problems with the previous approach:

1. The old implementation was misleadingly prefixed "sha256:" but
   actually used Rust's DefaultHasher (SipHash-1-3), which is not
   stable across Rust versions or platforms — golden checksums could
   silently break on compiler upgrades.

2. The old hasher only hashed cell character content, ignoring
   foreground color, background color, attribute flags, and link IDs.
   This meant two visually different frames could produce identical
   checksums, undermining the isomorphism proof guarantees.

The new BLAKE3 implementation:
- Hashes the complete visual state of each cell: content character,
  foreground color, background color, attribute flags, and link ID,
  all serialized in little-endian for cross-platform determinism
- Uses "blake3:" prefix (64 hex chars) instead of "sha256:" (16 hex)
- Provides cryptographic-strength collision resistance
- Is faster than SHA-256 on modern hardware

All 9 golden checksum files regenerated with new BLAKE3 hashes.
All test assertions updated to expect "blake3:" prefix and 64-char
hex digests. Beads issue descriptions updated to reflect the migration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
